title: vandellia maxwellii
intro: Vandellia maxwellii, also known as Kallare or Kalmegh, is a medicinal plant native to India and Southeast Asia, belonging to the family Loranthaceae. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antibacterial effects. The plant is known to offer various health benefits, such as reducing fever, alleviating digestive issues, and aiding in the treatment of respiratory problems like bronchitis and asthma. Vandellia maxwellii can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and decoctions, or used in combination with other herbs, to harness its medicinal properties.
